evals.prompt: Use $orchestrated-delivery to execute the existing package at docs/implementation/epics/E7/execution. It is marked ready_for_implementation. Stop after stories are merged back to the track branch unless I explicitly ask for PR publication.
evals.expected_output: The agent validates the package, binds runtime/provider facts, binds Codex custom-agent agent_type values at worker launch where supported, presents or follows an approved execution plan, creates per-story worktrees, dispatches packaged implementer and reviewer prompts in dependency waves under the same-logic rule, has each implementer commit every round in its story worktree, and on reviewer APPROVE merges each story's per-round commits back to the track branch and writes the tracker per the canonical schema, committing no story content itself, and stops before PR publication.
assertions: OD-1: The run starts only after finding an existing package marked ready_for_implementation.

OD-2: The agent records runtime/provider facts, including Codex agent_type where supported, without editing package-owned scope, prompts, ACs, dependency order, model class, effort, reasoning tier, or routing rationale.
OD-2: Codex agent_type is launch-time surface binding only and is not written into execution package artifacts.
OD-3: Dependency waves launch only after each prerequisite story is merged back to the track branch, its tracker row is merged, and its worker pair is closed.
OD-4: Each story reuses one implementer and one reviewer context; the implementer commits each round in its story worktree; workers never push, open PRs, merge, or close contexts.
OD-5: On reviewer APPROVE the orchestrator merges the story's per-round commits back to the track branch and updates the tracker; it commits no story content and does not re-grade the diff.
OD-6: The durable sequence per story is the implementer's per-round commits, then the track-branch merge-back, then the tracker update.
OD-7: The agent stops at the requested merge-back boundary before PR publication.
OD-8: Updates are sparse and evidence-based.

evals.prompt: Use $orchestrated-delivery on a ready package with one implementer prompt and one reviewer prompt. The current surface is Codex and supports custom agents. Bind runtime details before launch.
evals.expected_output: The skill records `agent_type: "implementer"` for the packaged implementer worker and `agent_type: "reviewer"` for the packaged reviewer worker as Codex launch-time bindings only. It keeps the package's abstract model class, effort, reasoning tier, routing rationale, provider profile, and concrete model resolution separate, and it does not write `agent_type` into plan.md, tracker.md, story contracts, DAGs, or packaged prompts.
assertions: OD-2: Codex agent_type is bound at runtime/launch only.
OD-2: Implementer prompts use agent_type implementer and reviewer prompts use agent_type reviewer on Codex surfaces that support custom agents.
OD-2: agent_type does not replace model class, effort, reasoning tier, provider profile, concrete model resolution, or routing rationale.
OD-2: agent_type is not written into package artifacts, story DAGs, story contracts, tracker rows, or packaged worker prompts.

evals.prompt: Use $orchestrated-delivery on this execution folder. It has plan.md and tracker.md, but no package-contained deep readiness verdict and one implementer prompt says to infer missing ACs from the code.
evals.expected_output: The agent refuses execution and routes the package back to plan-delivery or plan-epic as appropriate. It does not repair prompts, invent ACs, reorder stories, change model class or effort, or dispatch workers.
assertions: OD-1: Missing deep readiness evidence or incomplete prompts cause refusal.
OD-1: Structural file presence alone is not treated as readiness.
OD-2: The agent does not author or repair package-owned decisions.
OD-8: The refusal is concise and names the owning planning repair path.

evals.prompt: During $orchestrated-delivery a worker reports it pushed its story branch and opened a PR; separately the run is tempted to have the orchestrator stage and commit the approved story's source files itself before merge-back.
evals.expected_output: The skill treats the worker push/PR as a forbidden violation (the implementer commits each round only within its own story worktree and never pushes, opens PRs, merges, or closes), and forbids the orchestrator from committing story content: the orchestrator's only git writes are the track-branch merge-back of the implementer's per-round commits and the tracker.
assertions: OD-4: The implementer commits each round in its own story worktree but never pushes, opens PRs, merges, or closes contexts.
OD-4: A worker that pushed or opened a PR is treated as a violation, not accepted.
OD-5: The orchestrator commits no story content; on APPROVE it merges the implementer's per-round commits back to the track branch and writes the tracker.
OD-5: The orchestrator does not re-grade or override the reviewer's APPROVE.

evals.prompt: During $orchestrated-delivery, reviewer R1 returns blocking findings for story S1. The coordinator starts a new implementer I2 with the findings and then starts a new reviewer R2 to rereview the fix, because the copied context should be enough.
evals.expected_output: The skill rejects the replacement-worker loop. The orchestrator must message the existing implementer context with the exact findings (the implementer fixes, re-proves the gate, and commits the next round), then message the existing reviewer context to rereview that latest committed round. Replacement is allowed only after a recorded lost or unusable context exception and explicit orchestrator action.
assertions: OD-4: A story keeps exactly one implementer context and one reviewer context across fix/rereview rounds.
OD-4: Reviewer findings are sent to the existing implementer context rather than a fresh implementer.
OD-4: The fix is a new committed round sent to the existing reviewer context rather than a fresh reviewer.
OD-4: Replacement requires a recorded lost/unusable-context exception before any new worker is created.
OD-5: The reviewer reviews the latest committed round; the orchestrator routes findings without re-grading the diff.

evals.prompt: An implementer stops on story core-02-s2 and reports a source-contract blocker: ACs require policy-deny and approved-parent scope decisions, but the package exposes only policyRef and no approved-parent source. No usable code was produced.
evals.expected_output: The coordinator treats the report as a planning blocker, writes the affected tracker row blocked with the affected ACs, missing facts, and escalation/route-back target, creates no merge-back, does not unlock dependents, and routes repair to plan-epic or plan-delivery as appropriate.
assertions: OD-9: Source-contract blockers are treated as planning blockers rather than implementer failures.
OD-9: The coordinator does not ask the worker to invent missing policy or scope semantics.
OD-9: The affected tracker row records status blocked, blockers/notes with affected ACs, missing facts, worker alias, and route-back target.
OD-3: No dependent story is dispatched or unlocked because no merge-back exists on the track branch.
OD-6: No merge-back is created for a blocked story; blocked tracker evidence is stop evidence only and never unlocks dependents.

evals.prompt: During $orchestrated-delivery, story S2 reaches review round 5 and the reviewer still returns BLOCKING findings. Independent sibling stories S3 and S4 are mid-flight and do not depend on S2.
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator caps the loop at 5 rounds, blocks and escalates S2 to the architect, records the block in the tracker (round reached, per-round record, blocking AC/finding, escalation target), creates no merge-back for S2, keeps S2's dependents locked, blocks only the minimal set, and lets the non-dependent siblings keep running.
assertions: OD-10: The review loop is capped at 5 rounds; a fifth BLOCKING round triggers block + escalate rather than a sixth round.
OD-10: The escalation targets the architect and is recorded in S2's tracker row with the blocking AC/finding.
OD-10: No merge-back is created for the blocked story and its dependents stay locked.
OD-10: Only the minimal set is blocked; non-dependent sibling stories S3 and S4 keep running.

evals.prompt: During $orchestrated-delivery, story S5 is approved but the track branch advanced while it ran, so the --ff-only merge-back fails. In one variant the conflict is a trivial append-only overlap in the SDK barrel; in another it is a real logic conflict in a shared source file.
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator does not resolve story conflicts itself. It messages the persistent implementer to rebase S5 onto the current track HEAD and re-prove (gate green); a trivial replay rebases cleanly and the orchestrator then completes the merge-back. A real logic conflict means the same-logic rule was violated upstream — the orchestrator escalates it as a planning defect and does not silently resolve it.
assertions: OD-11: On a merge-back conflict the orchestrator requests an implementer rebase onto the track HEAD plus re-prove, not a self-resolution.
OD-11: A trivial replay (e.g. append-only barrel overlap) rebases cleanly and the orchestrator completes the track merge.
OD-11: A real logic conflict is escalated as an upstream planning defect and never silently resolved.
OD-5: The orchestrator's only git writes remain the track-branch merge-back and the tracker.

evals.prompt: During $orchestrated-delivery, the package schedules S6 and S7 in the same wave. They are non-dependent. S6 and S7 both write the same logic-bearing source file packages/core/src/policy.ts; separately S6 and S8 both append an export line to the SDK barrel packages/sdk/src/index.ts.
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator honors the same-logic rule from owned pathsets: S6 and S7 sharing a logic-bearing file must not run in the same wave unless the DAG records an architect override with a one-line rationale; sharing only the append-only SDK barrel does not block S6 and S8 from running concurrently — that overlap is resolved by rebase on merge-back, not by serializing the stories.
assertions: OD-12: Same-wave eligibility is checked at file-level from owned pathsets: two stories sharing a logic-bearing file are not run concurrently absent a recorded architect override.
OD-12: An architect override carrying a one-line rationale is the only exception that lets same-file stories share a wave.
OD-12: Stories sharing only an append-only aggregation point (the SDK barrel, registries, manifests, index/aggregator files) run concurrently and rebase on merge-back rather than being serialized.

evals.prompt: After all stories merged back to the track branch and a PR is open, a reviewer has approved the PR. The user has not issued any current merge instruction.
evals.expected_output: The skill refuses to merge, push, or perform any cleanup. It treats the PR review approval as detect-only, stops at the requested boundary, and waits for an explicit current merge instruction from the user before taking any further action.
assertions: OD-7: PR review waiting is detect-only; the skill does not merge without an explicit current user instruction.
OD-7: The skill does not push, merge, or clean up worker contexts after review approval absent a current instruction.
OD-7: The skill stops at the requested boundary and reports the PR state without acting on it.
OD-8: The closeout is sparse and evidence-based; no transcript or diff is dumped.

evals.prompt: Use $orchestrated-delivery on the ready package and open a PR (track branch to v-next) when all stories land. Do not wait for review, merge, or clean up.
evals.expected_output: The agent merges each approved story's per-round commits back to the track branch, opens or updates the PR from the track branch to v-next only after authorization, reports the PR URL, and stops without review waiting, merge, cleanup, or worker-context overreach.
assertions: OD-6: Stories are merged back to the track branch with tracker rows updated before publication.
OD-7: PR publication targets v-next, respects the requested boundary, and stops after reporting the URL.
OD-7: Review waiting is not started without explicit request.
OD-8: The closeout reports evidence rather than dumping transcripts or diffs.
